content: agent1: implementing click.edit() multi-file support. Will modify src/click/_termui_impl.py (Editor class) and src/click/termui.py (edit() function). Let me know if you're touching these files.

agent1: heads up, I already modified both files for multi-file edit support. In _termui_impl.py: renamed Editor.edit_file(self, filename: str) -> Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]), builds a quoted space-joined string for subprocess.Popen. In termui.py: click.edit() filename param is now str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None, added two @t.overload signatures above the edit() def, and the None-filename branch normalizes to a filenames tuple before calling ed.edit_files(filenames=filenames). Please add process_priority as an additional param/attribute rather than restructuring edit_files, and merge carefully around the Editor.__init__ and edit_files signatures so we don't clobber each other. Let me know when you're done so I can double check merge compatibility.

agent1: important - I renamed Editor.edit_file(self, filename) to Editor.edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]) (plural), since it now joins multiple quoted filenames before the single subprocess.Popen call. There is no edit_file() method anymore on my side. Current Editor.__init__ signature is unchanged from upstream: __init__(self, editor=None, env=None, require_save=True, extension='.txt'). Please rebase your process_priority changes onto edit_files() (add your popen_kwargs computation before the 'c = subprocess.Popen(f"{editor} {filenames}", env=environ, shell=True)' line, adding **popen_kwargs to that call), and add process_priority as the last param of __init__ as you planned - that's isolated from my change and fine. In termui.py, my edit() signature is: edit(text=None, editor=None, env=None, require_save=True, extension='.txt', filename: str | cabc.Iterable[str] | None = None) with two @t.overload decorators above it. Please add process_priority as the last kwarg after filename in both the overloads and the real def, and forward it to Editor(...). Ping me when updated so I can do a final sanity check before submitting my patch.

agent1: one more flag - you said 'edit_file() (untouched signature)' but that method no longer exists in my patch; I renamed/merged it into edit_files(self, filenames: cabc.Iterable[str]). If your popen_kwargs logic lives inside a method still named edit_file, it will silently not run (both edit() in termui.py and Editor.edit() call edit_files now). Please retarget your popen_kwargs computation into edit_files() and add **popen_kwargs to the 'c = subprocess.Popen(f"{editor} {filenames}", env=environ, shell=True)' call. This is the only place the subprocess is spawned now (single-file edits pass a 1-tuple). Just confirming so whoever merges our two patches doesn't end up with a dangling edit_file reference. My patch is finalized and written to patch.txt already - happy to help reconcile if useful.
